name: Pot Roast of Turkey with Barley
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat the oil in a large Dutch oven or roasting pan over high heat. Add the turkey, skin side down, and cook until the skin is golden brown, about 7 minutes.
2. Remove the turkey from the pot and set aside. Return the pot to the stove.
3. Add water, onion quarters with stems attached, minced garlic, bay leaves, fresh thyme, dried thyme, pearl barley, salt, and ground white pepper to the pot. Place the turkey back in the pot, skin side up.
4. Cover the pot and reduce heat to low. Simmer for 1 hour.
5. Meanwhile, melt butter over low heat in a covered skillet. Add the onion quarters with stems attached, cover, and cook for 20 minutes, stirring occasionally.
6. When the leeks are tender, remove from the heat and set aside.
7. Once the 1-hour simmering is complete, carefully remove the meat from the bones using tongs or a pair of kitchen shears. Discard the bones.
8. Cut the turkey breast into thin diagonal slices. Return the sliced meat to the pot with the barley mixture.
9. Stir in the whipping cream until it's incorporated.
10. When ready to serve, arrange the barley on a large platter. Place the turkey slices over the barley and pile the cooked leeks in the center of the platter. Serve immediately.
